from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is located in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    # Collect clause strings for all subjects to form a single complex sentence.
    all_clauses = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        parts = []

        # Preferred ordering of common attributes for readability.
        order = ["floorCount", "location", "height", "architect", "buildingType", "completionDate"]
        handled = set()

        # Helper to add a clause for a given predicate if present.
        def clean_obj(val: str) -> str:
            """Remove surrounding quotes and whitespace."""
            return val.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

        def add_clause(pred):
            for tr in group:
                if tr.predicate == pred:
                    obj = clean_obj(tr.object)
                    # Special handling for certain predicates
                    if pred == "buildingStartDate":
                        clause = f"Construction of {subject} began in {obj}"
                        handled.add("subject_introduced")
                    elif pred == "completionDate":
                        if "subject_introduced" in handled:
                            for i, part in enumerate(parts):
                                if part.startswith(f"Construction of {subject}"):
                                    parts[i] = part + f" and was completed in {obj}"
                                    break
                        else:
                            clause = f"Construction of {subject} was completed in {obj}"
                            handled.add("subject_introduced")
                    elif pred == "capital":
                        # Phrase as “<object> is the capital of <subject>”
                        clause = f"{obj} is the capital of {subject}"
                        handled.add("subject_introduced")
                    elif pred == "leader":
                        # Phrase as “<object> is the leader of <subject>”
                        clause = f"{obj} is the leader of {subject}"
                        handled.add("subject_introduced")
                    elif pred == "demonym":
                        clause = f"The demonym of {subject} is {obj}"
                        handled.add("subject_introduced")
                    elif pred == "language":
                        clause = f"One of the languages in {subject} is {obj}"
                        handled.add("subject_introduced")
                    elif pred == "country":
                        clause = f"{subject} is from {obj}"
                    elif pred in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                        noun = "floors" if pred == "floorCount" else ("beds" if pred == "bedCount" else "rooms")
                        clause = f"{verb_map.get(pred, 'has')} {obj} {noun}"
                    else:
                        verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
                        clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
                    parts.append(clause)
                    handled.add(pred)
                    break

        for pred in order:
            add_clause(pred)

        # Add remaining predicates that were not handled above.
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in handled:
                continue
            pred = tr.predicate
            obj = clean_obj(tr.object)

            # Special handling for predicates that require custom phrasing
            if pred == "leader":
                clause = f"{obj} is the leader of {subject}"
            elif pred == "capital":
                clause = f"{obj} is the capital of {subject}"
            elif pred == "language":
                clause = f"One of the languages in {subject} is {obj}"
            elif pred == "demonym":
                clause = f"The demonym of {subject} is {obj}"
            elif pred == "country":
                clause = f"{subject} is from {obj}"
            elif pred in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                noun = "floors" if pred == "floorCount" else ("beds" if pred == "bedCount" else "rooms")
                clause = f"{verb_map.get(pred, 'has')} {obj} {noun}"
            else:
                verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
                clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
            parts.append(clause)
            handled.add(pred)

        # Combine parts for this subject into a clause.
        clause = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts)
        all_clauses.append(clause)

    # Join all subject clauses into one sentence.
    final_text = "; ".join(all_clauses) + "."
    # Capitalize the first character of the whole output.
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
